text: Set Up the Event Challenge | HackerRank Knowledge Base Set Up the Event Challenge HackerRank Engage recommends a set of coding questions by default to help you quickly set up challenges for your event. To set up a challenge: Step 1: Review recommended questions To review recommended questions: Log in to your Engage account using your credentials. Log in to your Engage account using your credentials. Select the event for which you want to set up the challenge. Select the event for which you want to set up the challenge. In the left panel, select Challenges . In the left panel, select Challenges . Click the arrow icon next to a question to preview it. The question opens in a pop-up window. Click the arrow icon next to a question to preview it. The question opens in a pop-up window. Review all the recommended questions before publishing the challenge. Review all the recommended questions before publishing the challenge. Note: Use the Try Challenge option to test the questions before proceeding. If the recommended questions do not meet your requirements, contact the Engage team at engage-challenges@hackerrank.com . This notifies the Content, Professional Services, and Product teams at HackerRank. You can expect a response within one business day. Step 2: Review challenge settings By default, the challenge uses the centralized test settings configured in your account. When you publish a challenge, a consent prompt appears for enabling the recommended test integrity settings. To apply the recommended settings, select the checkbox labeled Enable the recommended integrity settings including those that make use of AI . The recommended test integrity settings include the following: Copy-paste tracking Copy-paste tracking Tab proctoring Tab proctoring Image proctoring with AI image analysis Image proctoring with AI image analysis Multiple monitor detection Multiple monitor detection AI-based plagiarism detection AI-based plagiarism detection Step 3: Publish challenge After reviewing the challenge and settings: Click Publish . Click Publish . In the confirmation pop-up up window, click Confirm to publish the challenge. In the confirmation pop-up up window, click Confirm to publish the challenge. Note: Once the microsite is published, you cannot change the participation type.To request changes, contact engage-challenges@hackerrank.com .
